rows.v: Juniper, citrus zest, pine, warm spice
Smoked salmon, oysters, charcuterie, sharp cheddar
Martinis, G&Ts — the benchmark everything else is measured against
Cucumber, rose, lavender, elderflower, fresh citrus
Light salads, sushi, soft cheeses, cucumber dishes
Gin skeptics — if you think you don't like gin, try this style first
Earthy juniper, light vanilla, honey, soft spice
Rich appetizers, pâté, aged cheeses
Not common on grocery shelves — specialty liquor stores or good cocktail bars. Great in a Tom Collins or Martinez.
Intense juniper, bold spice, more heat
Spicy food, bold flavors that can stand up to it
That's ~114 proof — nearly double a standard pour's ABV. Not a beginner's first glass neat; excellent in cocktails where you want the gin to cut through.
Gin infused with sloe berries and sugar — technically a liqueur
Sweet cherry, plum, almond, gentle spice
Desserts, chocolate, cheese boards
You'll see it on back bars looking like gin — it's sweeter and lower ABV. Great in a Sloe Gin Fizz. Don't order it expecting a standard gin drink.
London Dry gin, quality tonic water, lime or lemon
Crisp
Bitter
Refreshing
Ask for a premium tonic (Fever-Tree, Q Tonic) — cheap tonic is the #1 reason a G&T disappoints. The tonic is half the drink.
London Dry gin, dry vermouth
Elegant
Dry
Spirit-forward
If you're new to gin, order it "wet" — more vermouth softens the spirit. Most bars default to very dry, which is a lot of gin.
Gin, Campari, sweet vermouth (equal parts)
Bitter
Balanced
Complex
One of the great cocktails. If you find it too bitter at first, ask for a Negroni Sbagliato — same build but Campari + sweet vermouth topped with prosecco instead of gin. Lighter entry point.
London Dry gin, lemon juice, simple syrup, topped with Champagne
Elegant
Bright
Celebratory
One of the most elegant gin drinks and widely available. A great "I want gin but something approachable" order at any bar or restaurant.
Gin, fresh lime juice, simple syrup
Tart
Crisp
Clean
Ask for fresh lime, not Rose's lime cordial — the classic recipe used cordial but fresh is significantly better and most good bars will use it.
London Dry gin, lemon juice, simple syrup, soda water
Tall
Effervescent
Easy-drinking
Great gateway gin cocktail — the soda and lemon mellow the spirit considerably. Good "I'm not sure about gin yet" order.
Gin, maraschino liqueur, crème de violette, lemon juice
Floral
Vibrant
Violet-purple color
Stunning when made well, but crème de violette is uncommon — many bars skip it and the cocktail loses its character. Ask if they make a "proper Aviation" before ordering.
